emails.subject: Re: Northwestern
emails.body: Re: Northwestern How about "The Deal Might Not Happen LLC". In a call yesterday, Northwestern proposed changing the economic terms significantly, which would reduce our gross margin by more than half. We are awaiting a written proposal. It probably makes sense to go ahead anyway with the formation of the LLC, because we'll eventually need it even if this particular deal does not close. How about Merchant Energy Ventures, L.L.C. Let's catch up tomorrow. Regards, Ben Kay Mann@ENRON 03/12/2001 12:59 PM

emails.subject: Northwestern
emails.body: Folks: With respect to the Northwestern transaction, I'd like to understand what it would take in the letter agreement to allow us to recognize the entire $8MM upon execution of the letter agreement. In my conversation today with Kay, we talked about making sure we could at least recognize the $8MM in equal instalments between the execution of the letter agreement and September 1, 2001 (the outside date for closing). If at all possible, I'd like to take this one step further so that the entire amount could be recognized in Q2. Let's plan to talk about this on Monday. Any thoughts on this prior to Monday would be appreciated. Regards, Ben

emails.subject: Re: Northwestern
emails.body: Re: Northwestern I've raised some of the issues with NW. We are working on some of it. A meeting is a good idea. Kay From: Ben F Jacoby ECT 04/12/2001 04:20 PM Sent by: Ben Jacoby@ECT

emails.subject: Re: Northwestern
emails.body: Re: Northwestern Did we send Schroeder the Acknowledgement and Consent?

emails.subject: Re: Northwestern
emails.body: Bad Kay bad bad bad Kay. I'll do it now. ckm Ben F Jacoby@ECT 05/22/2001 03:11 PM Sent by: Ben Jacoby@ECT

emails.subject: RE: Northwestern
emails.body: RE: Northwestern I'm disappointed that GE didn't call. Must not have wanted to take the Enron credit risk. I think we will also need to report it to the merger legal team, which I will take care of once we know what is up. Kay To: Mann, Kay; Booth, Chris; Clark, Catherine

emails.subject: NorthWestern
emails.body: NorthWestern Ben, I need to get you to resign from the Montana Megawatts BOD. Will you be in this week? If not, we can handle it with fax/fed ex. Kay Kathleen, Please prepare a letter of resignation for Ben, leaving the date of the letter as November ___. Thanks.

emails.subject: RE: NorthWestern
emails.body: RE: NorthWestern I'm out (in SF) and off Wednesday. What's the status of the deal? Were we going to require them to get DOJ approval? I can be reached by fax today and tomorrow at 415-383-1613.

emails.subject: RE: NorthWestern
emails.body: Threshold is $50mm FMV of assets being sold. Don't think we can get to that point. Kay

emails.subject: RE: NorthWestern
emails.body: So when is this happening?

emails.subject: FW: Northwestern
emails.body: FW: Northwestern When do you think the payment's due?

emails.subject: RE: Northwestern
emails.body: Probably tomorrow. I got a feeling that Dave would like to visit with you about it. Dismal here. How are you?

emails.subject: FW: NorthWestern
emails.body: FW: NorthWestern Kay: Do you want to continue to hold off on the wire until the letter gets signed? FYI, Louise has given the OK to the wire.

emails.subject: RE: NorthWestern
emails.body: No need to hold off. I'm not worried about GE. I need Dave to sign one more document. Is he around? How do you like your new digs? Kay

emails.subject: RE: NorthWestern
emails.body: Dave is around. Digs are OK, but it's odd that all this money was spent, given our current circumstances.

emails.subject: RE: NorthWestern
emails.body: Dynegy is getting a very nice building. See you in a bit.

emails.subject: RE: NorthWestern
emails.body: Yes, they are getting a nice building, but will anyone still be here when they get it? Dave just dropped by. He's around until 2:30 today, and back tomorrow. Are you coming over?
